title: India · Small-Cap · Value & Quality · May 2026
universe: NSE 500 ∩ market cap < ₹20,000 Cr (lower mid + small cap)
methodologyNote: Five-phase funnel applied with a small-cap-specific tilt: stricter governance gate (promoter > 40%, no pledging, no audit qualifications), tighter liquidity floor (₹5 Cr ADV), and a hard skip on commodity-cyclical names at peak multiples. The goal is hidden-champion compounders, not turnaround plays.

quality: ROE 3y avg > 18% (raised from 15% for small caps)
D/E < 0.5 (stricter than 1.0 default — small caps with debt rarely survive cycles)
FCF+ in 4 of last 5 yrs (raised from 3 of 5)
Audit qualifications = none in last 3 yrs
Listed > 5 years (raised from 3 — avoid IPO-pop names)
valuation: P/E < 1.3× sector median OR < own 10y average
P/B not used — most small-cap quality names are asset-light
qualitative: Niche moat: regulatory duopoly, switching cost, IP, or category leadership in a sub-₹20k Cr addressable segment
Owner-operator: founder/family on the board, > 10y tenure
Capital allocation: history of dividends OR buybacks; sensible M&A only (no diworsification)
Industry: not commoditising; not at cyclical peak

exclusions.theme: Defense small-caps at 60-80× P/E (BEL, HAL adjacencies, MTAR, Paras Defense)
exclusions.reason: Peak-cycle multiples; order-book tailwind already extrapolated to 2030

exclusions.theme: Railway / capital goods small-caps (Titagarh, RVNL adjacencies)
exclusions.reason: PSU re-rating cycle late; mean reversion risk

exclusions.theme: Smallcap IT services (Tata Elxsi, Persistent peers below ₹20k Cr)
exclusions.reason: AI cannibalisation overhang — wait for valuation reset to play out

exclusions.theme: Renewables / solar EPC (Sterling & Wilson, Inox Wind)
exclusions.reason: Margin compression cycle; balance-sheet stress in peers
Credit-cycle turn underway; better to wait for the wash-out

exclusions.theme: QSR small caps (Westlife, Jubilant Foodworks small-cap peers)
exclusions.reason: Unit economics deteriorating; private competition compressing margins

exclusions.theme: Specialty chem cyclical names (Aarti, Navin Fluorine if at peak)
exclusions.reason: Cyclical price compression already visible; wait for capex digestion

picks.name: Vinati Organics Ltd.
picks.thesis: Global #1 in ATBS (~65% share) and IBB — niche specialty chem with hard-to-replicate process IP and a debt-free balance sheet.
picks.valueAngle: P/E ~32× vs 10y avg 38×; founder-led (Vinati Saraf Mutreja); butyl phenol & antioxidant downstream capacity ramping into FY27.

picks.name: Caplin Point Laboratories Ltd.
picks.thesis: LATAM-focused generics exporter with first-mover positions in Central/South America; recently scaling US injectables via Caplin Steriles.
picks.valueAngle: P/E ~22× with 25%+ ROCE, debt-free, FCF positive every year for a decade; US ramp is optional upside not priced in.

picks.name: Affle (India) Ltd.
picks.thesis: Mobile-first ad-tech platform with proprietary consumer-intent data set; CPC pricing model insulates from CPM compression in display.
picks.valueAngle: P/E ~50× looks rich but 4-yr revenue CAGR 30%+ at 25% EBITDA margin and net-cash balance sheet; international expansion (US, MENA) under-monetised.

picks.name: Suprajit Engineering Ltd.
picks.thesis: World's largest manufacturer of automotive control cables — supplies global 2W OEMs (Honda, Yamaha) plus Phoenix Lamps adjacency; hidden champion in a boring niche.
picks.valueAngle: P/E ~24× with 18% ROCE; EV-agnostic product (cables needed regardless of powertrain); recent Sundaram Industries cable-biz acquisition expands SAM.

picks.name: eClerx Services Ltd.
picks.thesis: Sticky digital-operations services to enterprise BFSI / cable / hi-tech clients; founder-promoted (Mundhra, Malik); aggressive buybacks reduce share count.
picks.valueAngle: P/E ~18× — cheapest of the listed Indian BPM/KPO peers; net-cash balance sheet; consistent 20%+ EBITDA margins despite India-IT wage inflation.

picks.name: Mold-Tek Packaging Ltd.
picks.thesis: In-Mold Labelling (IML) plastic packaging leader for India's paints, lubes, ghee, and ice-cream B2B customers — switching cost is real (tool moulds amortise over multi-year contracts).
picks.valueAngle: P/E ~22× with 20%+ ROCE, net-cash, 30%+ revenue from F&B (margin-rich); Birla Opus paint launch is a positive (not negative) for paint-packaging vendors.

thingsToWatch: Pledged-shares disclosures (zero today across all picks — re-check quarterly)
Promoter holding drift (small-cap promoter sales are an early bearish tell)
ADV liquidity for AFFLE and SUPRAJIT — if turnover drops below ₹3 Cr ADV, exit
Sectoral concentration in customer base (CAMS-AMC, ECLERX-Comcast, MOLDTKPAC-Asian Paints)
nextSteps: Run the value-analyst skill on each pick to produce a full 15-question deep dive. Suggested order: CAMS → VINATIORGA → CAPLIPOINT (highest-quality + most-asymmetric setups first).
